function_reachability: Is it impossible for function 'printf' to reach function 'main'?
loop_reachability: Is it possible that the loop with condition 'year < YEARS' is never executed?
Is the loop with condition 'year < YEARS' always executed?
Is it possible that the loop with condition 'month < MONTHS' is never executed?
Is the loop with condition 'month < MONTHS' always executed?
dominators: Is it guaranteed that whenever line 11:  <statement> {7.6, 5.6, 3.8, 2.8, 3.8, 0.2, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.3, 2.6, 5.2} </statement> is reached, the statement line 13:  <statement> int year, month; </statement> has already been reached?

Is it guaranteed that whenever line 20:  <statement> printf("%5d %15.1f\n", 2000 + year, subtot); </statement> is reached, the statement line 18:  <statement> for(month = 0, subtot = 0; month < MONTHS; month++) </statement> has already been reached?
data_dependency: Does the value of variable total at line 21: <statement> 	total += subtot; </statement> depend on the value of variable total at line 14: <statement>     float subtot, total; </statement>?

Does variable total at line 14: <statement>     float subtot, total; </statement> determine the value stored in variable subtot at line 19: <statement>             subtot += rain[year][month]; </statement>?
liveness: Does variable 'total' remain in use at the end of function 'main'?
Is variable 'month' live at the end of function 'main'?
dead_code: Can the statement 'total += subtot;' be considered dead code?
